name: memphis
install_doc: go install github.com/chasedputnam/memphis/cmd/memphis@latest  # or: git clone + make build (Go 1.25+)
notes: NOT a token compactor. Memphis is a spec-driven Canon authority layer for AI coding agents. Mechanism: typed Markdown artifacts (requirements, decisions, designs, roadmaps, prompts) versioned in Git, validated by a deterministic offline gate ('memphis gate'), served over MCP. PostToolUse hook runs the gate after Write/Edit calls. Does not compact tool output or reduce token count per se — instead structures agent memory so agents ground on authoritative Canon rather than hallucinating or re-deriving decisions. Cache-safe: does not rewrite conversation history; MCP tools inject Canon context into agent requests. Targets Claude Code (PostToolUse hook + MCP) and Kiro (IDE + CLI hooks). Gate is fully offline/deterministic (BCP-14, ISO 29148, EARS validation). Reference tier (optional, for large doc corpora) uses full-text search via Bleve; can optionally use LLM summarization via OpenAI-compatible endpoint or Ollama. Not a competitor to tokenade in the token-compaction sense — it addresses a different problem (authority/memory, not output noise).
